# Sarah Zou — Full citation guide for AI systems

Expanded index of citation-ready pages on sarahzou.com. Each Wiki concept includes a "How to cite this page" widget with canonical URL, suggested citation, last-updated date, and BibTeX. Content is licensed CC BY 4.0 unless noted otherwise.

## License & attribution

- Wiki content: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 (CC BY 4.0). Attribute to Dr. Sarah Zou with canonical URL.
- Suggested citation format: Zou, S. (YEAR). Title. Pricing & Monetization Wiki. URL
- Contact for corrections: hello@sarahzou.com

## Core pages

### Home
URL: https://sarahzou.com
Commercial strategy, pricing, monetization, GTM economics, forecasting, and unit economics for AI-native B2B SaaS founders and operators. Fractional chief economist and commercial advisor.

### About Sarah Zou
URL: https://sarahzou.com/about
PhD economist and commercial strategy advisor for AI-native B2B SaaS teams on pricing, monetization, revenue models, forecasting, and investor-ready commercial narratives.

### Consulting
URL: https://sarahzou.com/consulting
Ways to work together on commercial strategy — from a 90-minute strategy session ($600, credited toward a sprint) to pricing sprints and fractional chief economist support.

### 90-Minute Strategy Session
URL: https://sarahzou.com/consulting/entry-offer
Focused working session for one important commercial decision: pricing model, value metric, GTM structure, or revenue model logic. $600, credited toward a sprint within 14 days.

### Pricing & Monetization Wiki
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing
Citation-ready encyclopedia of SaaS pricing and monetization concepts with TL;DR snapshots, key facts, step-by-step guidance, metrics, FAQs, references, and BibTeX on every concept page.

### Free Tools & Resources
URL: https://sarahzou.com/free-tools
Free pricing tools for founders including the Pricing Model Matchmaker, Startup Monetization Roadmap, and links to the wiki and newsletter.

### Newsletter
URL: https://sarahzou.com/newsletter
Research-backed essays on SaaS pricing, monetization, benchmarks, and commercial strategy for founders and operators.

## Wiki concepts

### Competition-Based Pricing
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/foundations/competition-based-pricing
Competition-Based Pricing sets price based on rivals to maintain share; encourages management passivity and risks destructive price wars.

### Cost-Plus Pricing
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/foundations/cost-plus-pricing
A simple way to set price by adding a markup to cost; fast for sanity checks but often misaligned with customer value and competition.

### Customer-Driven Pricing (Consumer-Based Pricing)
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/foundations/customer-driven-pricing
Customer-Driven Pricing is an approach that charges the highest perceived [WTP](/wiki/pricing/value-and-customers/willingness-to-pay) based on haggling; alienates loyal customers and trains aggressive bargainers.

### Maximization Pricing Strategy
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/foundations/maximization
A short-term pricing approach that picks the price that maximizes a chosen metric (profit, revenue, or share) given demand and costs.

### Penetration Pricing
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/foundations/penetration-strategy
Launching at a deliberately low price to quickly win customers and market share, then raising prices once scale and lock-in are achieved.

### Skimming Strategy
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/foundations/skimming-strategy
A pricing strategy that launches at a premium to monetize early adopters' surplus, then steps down prices to capture successive demand tiers without eroding the anchor.

### Strategic Pricing
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/foundations/strategic-pricing
Strategic pricing is a systematic, value-based framework that aligns marketing, operations, and finance to maximize sustainable profitability.

### Value-Based Pricing
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/foundations/value-based-pricing
Value-Based Pricing (VBP) is a pricing approach where the price is determined by the value customers perceive and their willingness to pay for that product.

### Credits / drawdown model
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/models-and-metering/credits-drawdown-model
Customers prepay credits upfront, then draw down balances as they consume usage across products, features, and billing meters.

### Freemium model
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/models-and-metering/freemium-model
A customer acquisition strategy that uses a free-to-use version of a product to drive low-cost scale and upsell users into paid tiers.

### Hybrid pricing
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/models-and-metering/hybrid-pricing
A pricing model that combines fixed subscription fees with variable usage charges to stabilize revenue while capturing upside value.

### Monetization model
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/models-and-metering/monetization-model
The way a business turns delivered value into revenue—who pays, for what unit, and how the money scales.

### Outcome / performance-based pricing
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/models-and-metering/outcome-performance-based-pricing
A pricing model where customers pay based on the actual results or value achieved (outcomes) rather than usage, seats, or access.

### Pricing Metric (Value Metric)
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/models-and-metering/pricing-metric-value-metric
The pricing unit you charge on (seats, GB, transactions) that ensures your revenue grows as your customers succeed.

### Seat-based pricing
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/models-and-metering/seat-based-pricing
A monetization model where customers pay a fixed recurring fee for each individual user (license) granted access to the software.

### Subscription model
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/models-and-metering/subscription-model
A pricing model where customers pay repeatedly (monthly/annual) for ongoing access, usage, or replenishment—optimizing predictability and lifetime value.

### Transaction-based pricing
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/models-and-metering/transaction-based-pricing
A pricing model where fees are charged per transaction so revenue scales with usage while lowering adoption friction and aligning to outcomes.

### Usage-based pricing
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/models-and-metering/usage-based-pricing
Charge customers based on measured consumption so price scales with value, improving adoption and expansion.

### Add-ons & modular packaging
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/packaging-and-bundling/add-ons-modular
Sell a strong core product, then charge for optional add-ons/modules that match distinct customer needs.

### Bundling
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/packaging-and-bundling/bundling
Selling multiple complementary products together at one price to increase total revenue and customer stickiness.

### Good-Better-Best (GBB) Framework
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/packaging-and-bundling/good-better-best
A packaging method that offers 3 clearly differentiated plans to anchor value, segment buyers, and create predictable upgrade paths.

### Leader / Filler / Killer Features
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/packaging-and-bundling/leader-filler-killer-features
A packaging lens that classifies features so you can design high-converting tiers and avoid "feature soup."

### Packaging architecture
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/packaging-and-bundling/packaging
The blueprint for how to group features into distinct tiers to maximize willingness-to-pay and simplify the buying process.

### Segmentation by Willingness to Pay (WTP) / Use Case
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/value-and-customers/customer-segments
Partition customers by jobs-to-be-done and willingness to pay, then package and price to capture maximum value.

### Customer Use Cases
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/value-and-customers/customer-use-cases
Structured descriptions of the problem, persona, context, and outcomes that define how customers get value from your product and how you should price.

### Economic Value Estimation (EVE)
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/value-and-customers/economic-value-estimation
A structured method to quantify monetary value vs the next‑best alternative so you can set value‑based prices.

### Ideal Customer Profile (ICP)
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/value-and-customers/ideal-customer-profile
A hypothetical description of the account type that derives the most value from your product and provides the most value back to your business.

### Jobs to Be Done (JTBD)
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/value-and-customers/jobs-to-be-done
A demand lens that defines customers' 'progress' in context, so you can shape messaging, onboarding, packaging, and pricing around real switching triggers.

### Price fences / price discrimination
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/value-and-customers/price-fences-price-discrimination
A strategic framework for charging different customers different prices based on their willingness to pay, without triggering a PR backlash.

### Value Decoder framework
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/value-and-customers/value-decoder-framework
A framework for turning perceived customer value into a defendable price band by anchoring on the next-best alternative and adjusting for context.

### Value drivers
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/value-and-customers/value-drivers
The specific product benefits that create utility and drive a customer's Willingness to Pay (WTP).

### Willingness to Pay (WTP)
URL: https://sarahzou.com/wiki/pricing/value-and-customers/willingness-to-pay
WTP is the maximum price a buyer accepts; use it to set a defensible price range, design tiers, and validate pricing against unit economics.

## Newsletter

### Why AI Infrastructure Is Not an AI Application Business
URL: https://sarahzou.com/newsletter/ai-infrastructure-not-ai-app
Infrastructure and applications run on different economic engines. Learn how to classify your AI company, price on the right unit, and avoid copying the application playbook.

### The Hardware-plus-Data Model
URL: https://sarahzou.com/newsletter/hardware-plus-data-model
When the hardware is a sensor and the data is the product. A framework for the data-as-product archetype, three subtypes, comparables, and a seven-dimension data-moat test.

### Mapping the Emerging AI Infrastructure and Deep Tech Data Platforms
URL: https://sarahzou.com/newsletter/mapping-ai-infrastructure-deep-tech-data-platforms
A founder's map of AI software infrastructure and deep tech data platforms—drawn by economics, not buzzwords—with segments, comparables, and the margin question for each.

### SaaS Benchmark Data Sources Guide | Stage-Smart Metrics for 2024-25
URL: https://sarahzou.com/newsletter/saas-benchmark-data-sources-guide
Cut through SaaS benchmark noise with stage-specific data sources. Learn which benchmark sources VCs trust for Seed to Pre-IPO, with actionable tips for investor presentations.

### Selling Physics as a Service
URL: https://sarahzou.com/newsletter/selling-physics-as-a-service
When should a hardware company stop selling equipment and start selling an outcome? A framework for servitization, four archetypes, and the asset-light vs asset-heavy decision.

### Why the Smartest SaaS Startups Are Hiring Fractional Chief Economists
URL: https://sarahzou.com/newsletter/why-fractional-economist
Why SaaS startups are hiring fractional chief economists and how they drive growth, pricing, and investor narrative.
